predicted.topic_label: Perfect Day Mexico capex and economics

predicted.question: Hi. Good morning. Michael — Legend of the Seas is in a strong book position with prices higher than Icon and Star per Jason's comments; can you quantify the pricing premium (APD) and booked load factor you are seeing for Legend in Europe versus the Caribbean, and what incremental APD or margin uplift you expect when Legend redeploys to the Caribbean in November?
predicted.topic_label: Legend pricing premium / APD and redeployment

predicted.question: Hi. Good morning. Naftali — you flagged a $0.62 per-share fuel drag (or $0.74 incl. JVs) and said forward consumption is ~59% hedged; can you walk us through the per-share sensitivity you used (e.g., $X of EPS per $10 change in fuel), confirm current hedge coverage and tenor, and comment on whether you intend to add incremental hedges given current spot-forward dynamics?

Hi. Good morning. Naftali — you said you issued a $2.5 billion investment-grade bond this quarter and used proceeds to refinance near-term maturities; can you provide the coupon and tenor, how proceeds were allocated between refinancing and new-build/destination capex, and the pro forma effect on FY2026 net leverage and annual interest expense?
predicted.topic_label: Bond proceeds allocation and leverage impact

predicted.question: Hi. Good morning. Naftali — you highlighted an increase in younger guests (millennials and younger) and more repeat guests; can you give us the current share of sailings made up by millennials/Gen Z and how that cohort shift has materially changed onboard mix (e.g., proportion of F&B, experiences, shore excursions booked pre-cruise) versus a year ago?

Hi. Good morning. Jason — you reiterated Perfecta's 20% adjusted EPS CAGR through 2027 and noted fuel and the TUI JV as near-term headwinds; is FY2026 still on-track to deliver the stated CAGR before and after the $0.74 headwind, and when should we expect management to lay out a Post-Perfecta financial framework (targets and time horizon)?

Hey, good morning — Naftali, you mentioned you accessed the markets via a $2.5 billion investment‑grade bond and that net proceeds refinanced near‑term maturities; are you able to walk us through the pricing (coupon) and tenor of that issuance and then — given the choice between unsecured bonds and ECA‑backed ship financing — were you economically indifferent on a blended all‑in cost basis or were there other strategic benefits that drove the decision?

Naftali — following that $2.5 billion bond and your comment that liquidity is $6.9 billion and leverage is below 3x, can you give us the pro‑forma FY‑2026 net leverage and expected run‑rate interest expense after the transaction, and second, does that pro‑forma position change how you prioritize the remaining $1 billion of buyback capacity versus further deleveraging?

Hey — quick one on buybacks versus deleveraging: you repurchased $836 million this quarter and have ~$1 billion left; as you look forward, what's the priority order between opportunistic repurchases and accelerating paydown to target an IG 'headroom' metric, and at what net‑leverage threshold would you materially slow buybacks?

Naftali — you called out fuel as a roughly $0.62 per‑share headwind (or $0.74 incl. JVs) and said forward consumption is ~59% hedged and guidance is based on spot; would you consider changing your hedging posture (i.e., increasing coverage or layering) to reduce EPS volatility, and what trade‑offs (cost versus flexibility) would you be weighing in that decision?

Naftali — on costs: you said Net Cruise Costs ex‑fuel are expected to be approximately flat for the year but that first‑half cost growth is higher because dry docks and timing create front‑loaded pressure (Q2 NCC ex‑fuel up 4.6%–5.1% with ~400 bps headwinds); at what capacity‑growth level or run‑rate do you think NCC ex‑fuel will normalize back toward a 2%–3% inflationary range?

Naftali — on the intra‑year cadence you highlighted (Q2 bearing almost 400 bps of dry‑dock and travel impacts and Q2 Net Yield only ~0.2% after ~200 bps headwind), how much of those Q2/Q3 cost and yield hits are purely timing versus structural (e.g., ongoing island/destination run‑rate), and specifically are any Costa Maya / Perfect Day Mexico ramp costs already embedded in the 2026 NCC ex‑fuel outlook?

Jason / Naftali — you reiterated Perfecta's 20% adjusted EPS CAGR to 2027 and called the program 'on track' despite calling out a $0.74 fuel/JV headwind; can you walk through how much of that headwind is expected to be absorbed organically by the Perfecta levers (new hardware, destinations, AI) versus relying on financial engineering, and does the recent bond / buyback activity change your confidence in hitting the 2027 EPS target?

Jason / Naftali — you announced Icon 6 and Icon 7 orders and reiterated strong returns from the Icon platform; two quick things — what financing mix do you intend to use for those incremental Icon ships (will you lean ECA or unsecured markets) and what is the incremental ROIC or per‑ship return assumption you bake into Perfecta for each additional Icon class delivery?

Michael — you called Legend of the Seas 'in a strong book position with prices higher than Icon and Star' and plan a Europe summer debut before redeploying to the Caribbean — do you expect Legend's European pricing premium to hold through the summer, and can you quantify the expected Net Yield or APD delta when Legend moves to the Caribbean in November (and the implications for the Q4 like‑for‑like comp)?

Naftali — last one on the bond program: given the strong oversubscription you cited, does this change your expected cadence of accessing the investment‑grade market through 2027 for ship financings or corporate needs, and should we assume a lower blended cost of debt for future new‑build funding versus your historical ECA mix?
predicted.topic_label: future IG access & cost of debt

Good morning Naf �������— Net Yield is guided to 1.5%-2.5% for the year and you said Q1 Net Yield was +2%; can you decompose the FY Net Yield midpoint into ticket vs onboard and quantify the incremental contribution from new hardware/private destinations (e.g., Icon/Star and CocoCay/Perfect Day Mexico)?

Thanks, good morning — Naftali, Q1 Net Yield came in at +2% and you reiterated full-year Net Yield of 1.5%-2.5%; can you walk me through a quantitative decomposition to the FY midpoint showing ticket vs. onboard, like‑for‑like vs. new‑hardware/private‑destination (Icon/Perfect Day Mexico) contributions and regional bps (Med vs Caribbean) that underlie that guide?

Thanks, good morning — Naftali, you said Q2 Net Yield is ~+0.2% and that year‑over‑year comparisons and geopolitical events create almost a 200bp headwind in the quarter; can you quantify that ~200bps split between itinerary/geopolitical effects (Mediterranean + West Coast Mexico), dry‑dock/APCD timing, and assumed pricing/backfill?

Thanks, good morning — Naftali, you noted ~60% fuel hedged for 2026, fuel expense of $1.35B and a $0.62/share fuel headwind; what's your sensitivity to a 10% and 20% sustained move in bunker spot on FY Adjusted EPS (and EPS excluding JVs), and are you considering incremental hedging given you said fuel expense would be ~4% lower using the forward curve?

Thanks, good morning — Naftali, Net Cruise Costs ex‑fuel are now described as approximately flat for the year but Q2 NCC ex‑fuel is up 4.6%-5.1% with almost 400bps of headwinds — can you break out structural versus timing items (dry‑dock cadence, Hideaway/Perfect Day Mexico ramp, crew travel/air disruptions) and say when you’d expect NCC ex‑fuel to normalize?

Thanks, good morning — Michael, you said Legend is 'in a strong book position with prices higher than Icon and Star' in Europe; can you quantify the booked APD and load factor for Legend's European summer and the expected yield delta when it redeploys to the Caribbean in November (and the resulting impact to Q4 comps)?

Thanks, good morning — Jason, you reaffirmed Perfect Day Mexico opening late‑2027 and compared Costa Maya to 'roughly the size of Magic Kingdom'; can you give us expected per‑guest ancillary spend at maturity and whether the ticket vs onboard revenue mix will look more like CocoCay or be a more balanced split?

Thanks, good morning — Jason, you announced orders for Icon 6 and Icon 7 and said the Icon platform delivers 'superior economics' — can you share the targeted incremental Adjusted EBITDA per APCD or targeted ROIC for each incremental Icon versus the smaller Discovery class you referenced as the follow‑on?

Thanks, good morning — Naftali, you executed a $2.5B investment‑grade bond that was 'significantly oversubscribed' and used proceeds to refinance near‑term maturities; can you disclose the coupon and tenor, how proceeds split between refinancing and new‑build/destination CapEx, and the pro‑forma effect on FY2026 net leverage and cash interest?

Thanks, good morning — Jason, you noted the Royal ONE launch, app MAUs are 5x 2019 and that 'more than half of onboard revenue is booked before guests step on board'; how should we model the Royal ONE and higher pre‑cruise booking penetration translating into APD lift and incremental margin over 2026–27?

Thanks, good morning — Naftali, you returned $1.1B in the quarter, repurchased $836M and have $1B remaining authorization with $6.9B liquidity — given ongoing new‑build and destination CapEx, can you describe the prioritization framework and the expected cadence for using the remaining buyback authorization versus funding new‑builds/destination investments over the next 12–18 months?

actual.question: Great. Thanks. Jason, maybe if we take a step back. Despite geopolitical developments and the elevated industry capacity in the Caribbean, your yield guide at the high end this year stands at 2.5% constant currency. Maybe could you speak to the drivers of durable growth multi-year, which seem intact here regardless of the macro, and just how you see the company set up today relative to pre-pandemic?

Yeah. Hey, guys. Good morning. Thanks for all the color so far. Jason, as we think about the rest of the year, we obviously have your second quarter yield guidance, and I have to assume based on Naftali's comments that your third quarter yields are gonna look somewhat similar to your second quarter given the exposure you have to Europe. You know, then if that's true, that would imply your fourth quarter yields are gonna be growing, you know, let's call it somewhere in that mid-single- digit range to kinda get you into that 2%, you know, midpoint. You know, wondering what gives you the confidence the fourth quarter could grow that much. I guess then that actually to me would imply, that without the European headwinds you guys encountered, you know, you guys would've actually been able to raise your full year yield guidance. Am I kinda thinking about that all the right way?

Great. Thanks for taking my question. I just wanted to circle back on the third quarter and the Med, and just maybe if you could put a bit of a finer point on it. You know, how much do you have left to book at this point in the year? How much damage do you think was done over the last few weeks? What are you sort of baking into your forward guidance in terms of how the conflict plays out and how bookings play out from here?

Good morning, thanks for taking my questions. I wanted to sort of zoom in on the idea that we're turning the corner. Obviously, you know, the weeks following the initial geopolitical disruption were probably the worst, but maybe some indication of where we stand today in terms of the booking trajectory versus where we were in February before a lot of this started. I don't know if we're fully back or we're just heading in that direction. As we think about sort of the 2Q and 3Q, we're saying that's most pronounced. I'm just curious if that's because those are what's next or whether consumers are comfortable booking beyond the third quarter and into 2027, assuming that, you know, this disruption will go away or we'll sort of worry about that when we get to that point in time. Thanks.

Hi. Good morning. Thanks for taking the question. I was wondering if you could maybe give us a refresh on Perfect Day Mexico. You mentioned it's opening late 2027, ramping 2028. Could you maybe share some more details on the cadence of that ramp? You know, just bigger picture, your latest thinking around the long-term, you know, structural yield growth opportunity there in the Western Caribbean market and particularly around, you know, the Galveston, Texas penetration opportunity.

Thanks. I had a question on yields, but also just a quick follow-up. Michael's comment may have just answered it, but, you know, it sounded like Mexico there had been, like, a little bit of a pause in construction because of that environmental stuff. Just wanna clarify if Michael's comment means that construction is, you know, resumed in Mexico there.

Great. Thank you. Thank you. The other question was just sort of thinking about next year and, you know, if it's the 200 basis points impact in Q2, Q3 sounds like, you know, the entire 100 basis point change is maybe a mid-single digit sort of shift in where you had expected European yields to come, to come in this year. Is it fair to assume that you would kind of fully expect that to come back in 2027 when we're kind of thinking ahead to, you know, the impact this year being kind of, you know, not necessarily coming out of next year? Just help us to size that. Thank you.

Thanks. Just wanted to dig a little bit more into yield outlook for the year. Could you maybe comment on how you think new hardware, you have Star , Xcel contribution, Paradise Island, RBC Santorini, like a lot of, you know, exciting new products out there, how they might be contributing to the yield growth overall versus the like- for-L ike impact. Also on a regional basis, I think you had mentioned or used the term that Europe was doing well, I think was the quote. Is it fair to assume that Europe yields will grow this year? What does the guide assume? Thanks.

Hi. Thanks for taking the question. I guess I wanted to follow up on cost. Are you making any itinerary changes given higher fuel either currently or looking out to 2027, 2028? Obviously, Net Cruise Costs are coming in a bit lower. Is there anything you've pulled back on this year in terms of initiatives or spend that we should think of as deferring to 2027? Is this just harvesting some of those efficiencies that you just referred to? Thank you.

Hi, good morning, everyone. T wo quick questions on costs, you know, so I guess for Naftali. I guess, one, you know, how do you think of rolling in new hedges in this high fuel environment? Second, just on unit costs. You continue to do a really nice job here. I guess my question is, at what level of capacity growth would we start to see maybe more inflationary type, you know, NCC ex-fuel growth and say, I don't know, 2%-3% range? Just curious your thoughts up there. Thank you.

Hi, guys. Thanks for the question. You talked about the co-branded credit card and several changes to the loyalty program, also how repeat guests are kind of stepped up. I'm kind of wondering what do you think is kind of the implications of that in terms of how they could impact net yield growth, maybe repeat guests are booking further ahead, maybe they spend more on onboard. Kind of any learnings on how, you know, higher repeat penetration could be. Thank you.

Great. Thanks a lot. I had a question on North American customers and higher airfares. Can you talk at all, you know, have you seen any consumer behavior change at all kind of reacting to the higher airfares in North America for your North America itineraries? How do you see consumers' ability to kind of swallow those airfare increases as they're getting to ports as the year goes on? Thanks.
